2025-04-25.abstract: Two legal actions seeking U.S. government intervention say that some of the coffee bought by major American retailers is harvested in conditions that amount to slavery.

2025-04-25.title: Where Bruce Lee Practiced on the Roof, a Shrine to Old Hong Kong Rises
2025-04-25.abstract: At a formerly grand hotel where the famed martial arts star once stayed, a group of collectors is trying to preserve vestiges of the city’s past as its political identity changes.

2025-04-25.title: Pope Francis Simplified Papal Funerals. Not Everyone Will Notice.
2025-04-25.abstract: The changes reflect Francis’ view of the pope as a pastor more than a powerful person, but the rites will still be on a grand scale.

2025-04-25.title: Air Raids and Antidepressants: Sleep Deprivation Takes a Toll in Ukraine
2025-04-25.abstract: Sleep deprivation has become a health crisis in the country, experts and psychologists say. They cite near-nightly drone attacks as one of the major impediments to getting proper rest.

2025-04-25.title: A Faithful Throng Pays Last Respects to Pope Francis
2025-04-25.abstract: “It meant a lot to me to see him, he gave us so much,” said one woman who waited in line to file past the pontiff’s casket.

2025-04-26.title: Iran-U.S. Nuclear Talks: What’s at Stake?
2025-04-26.abstract: A new proposal by the Trump administration would allow Iran to continue enriching uranium at low levels while a broader arrangement is worked out.

2025-04-26.title: As Tensions Rise With Pakistan, a Moment of Truth for India’s Military
2025-04-26.abstract: The risk of exposing a military still being modernized may constrain Prime Minister Narendra Modi as he weighs retaliation for a terrorist attack.

2025-04-26.title: Security is tight at St. Peter’s Square and across Rome.
Who Is Sitting Where at Pope Francis’ Funeral?
2025-04-26.abstract: With dozens of foreign delegations attending, some from hostile countries, the funeral’s seating arrangements presented a possible nightmare for Vatican planners.

2025-04-27.title: How Foreign Students Lost Their Sheen in a Nation of Immigrants
2025-04-27.abstract: Both major political parties are pledging steep cuts on the number of foreigners allowed to study in Australia as a way to rein in runaway housing prices.

2025-04-27.title: Europe’s Pharma Industry Braces for Pain as Trump Tariff Threat Looms
2025-04-27.abstract: Medicines and chemicals are huge exports for European Union countries. That makes the sector a weak spot as trade tensions drag on.

2025-04-27.title: As Cardinals Prepare to Elect a Pope, One Motto Is ‘Unity.’ That’s Divisive.
2025-04-27.abstract: Some see the byword as a rallying call in a conservative campaign to reverse Francis’ push for a more inclusive church.

2025-04-27.title: India Seems to Be Building Its Case for Striking Pakistan
2025-04-27.abstract: As world powers face multiple crises, the one set off by a terror attack in Kashmir is getting scant attention or help in de-escalating between nuclear-armed neighbors.

2025-04-27.abstract: Dozens of people were also reported injured after a car drove into a Filipino community festival in British Columbia, the police said.

2025-04-28.title: ​Putin Thanks Kim for North Korean Troops Fighting Against Ukraine
2025-04-28.abstract: North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, ordered a monument for the “heroic” soldiers killed in Russia’s war against Ukraine, as Moscow and Pyongyang make first comments on the joint operation.

2025-04-28.title: One Key to a Successful Campaign for Pope? Act Like You’re Not Campaigning.
2025-04-28.abstract: Crucial meetings will be held this week in which contenders begin jockeying in earnest for the job of leading the Roman Catholic Church.

2025-04-28.title: As India and Pakistan Cancel Visas, Parents and Children Are Separated
2025-04-28.abstract: The repercussions from a terrorist attack led to painful scenes at the countries’ border, as families with mixed citizenship were suddenly divided.

2025-04-28.title: China and the Philippines Spar Over a Sandbar in the South China Sea
2025-04-28.abstract: The standoff over Sandy Cay, which lies close to military outposts built by both Beijing and Manila, was the latest between the two nations.

2025-04-28.title: Canada Votes: What’s at Stake?
2025-04-28.abstract: Many Canadians believe Monday’s election is the most important of their lifetime. It will determine who will take on a stagnant economy and deal with President Trump.

2025-04-29.title: When will we know the results?
Despair Blankets Scene of Car-Ramming Attack at Festival
2025-04-29.abstract: Filipinos in Vancouver returned to a neighborhood to mourn the 11 people killed in a weekend attack.

2025-04-29.title: Mark Carney Wins New Term as Canada’s Prime Minister on Anti-Trump Platform
2025-04-29.abstract: The election was dominated by Mr. Trump and his relentless focus on Canada. Pierre Poilievre, the opposition leader, lost the seat he had held for 20 years.

2025-04-29.title: Trump’s Tariffs Put China’s E-Commerce Superpowers to the Test
2025-04-29.abstract: Companies like Alibaba that built China’s world-leading online shopping sector are now helping its sellers find markets beyond the United States.

2025-04-29.title: A Contender for the Papacy in the Mold of Francis
2025-04-29.abstract: Cardinal Luis Tagle of the Philippines is known as the “Asian Francis.” But he has been criticized for not being vocal enough about his country’s brutal drug war and clerical sex abuse.

2025-04-30.title: U.N. Orders Agencies to Find Budget Cuts, Including via Staff Relocations From N.Y.
2025-04-30.abstract: The instructions from the office of Secretary General António Guterres were reviewed by The New York Times and came after President Trump ordered a review of U.S. funding to the agency.

2025-04-30.title: Mark Carney Swept Canada, but There Will Be No Honeymoon
2025-04-30.abstract: The Canadian prime minister achieved a stunning political upset, running on an anti-Trump platform and promising to revive the economy. Now, he needs to deliver.

2025-04-30.abstract: Pierre Poilievre lost the vote for a constituency he has held for 21 years to a Liberal political neophyte. His populist approach may have been to blame.

2025-04-30.title: A U.S. Businessman Is Sentenced to 5 Years in Prison in China
2025-04-30.abstract: The charges stemmed from a criminal case more than 20 years ago. In the interim, the businessman, David Lee, had repeatedly visited China without issue.

2025-04-30.title: Families Are Split as Pakistan Deports Thousands of Afghan Refugees
2025-04-30.abstract: Many undocumented Afghan migrants have Pakistani spouses and have lived in the country for years. Nevertheless, the government says they must leave.
